heading: Funding for harbor maintenance programs
label: 2238b Funding for harbor maintenance programs
notes: CodificationSection was enacted as part of the Water Resources Reform and Development Act of 2014, and not as part of the Water Resources Development Act of 1986 which comprises this chapter.

children.heading: Total amount of harbor maintenance taxes received
children.label: 1 Total amount of harbor maintenance taxes received
children.text: The term “total amount of harbor maintenance taxes received” means, with respect to a fiscal year, the aggregate of amounts appropriated, transferred, or credited to the Harbor Maintenance Trust Fund under for that fiscal year as set forth in the current year estimate provided in the President’s budget request for the subsequent fiscal year, submitted pursuant to .section 9505(a) of title 26section 1105 of title 31

children.heading: Total budget resources
children.label: 2 Total budget resources
children.text: The term “total budget resources” means the total amount made available by appropriations Acts from the Harbor Maintenance Trust Fund for a fiscal year for making expenditures under .section 9505(c) of title 26

children.label: A
children.text: For fiscal year 2015, 67 percent of the total amount of harbor maintenance taxes received in fiscal year 2014.

children.label: B
children.text: For fiscal year 2016, 69 percent of the total amount of harbor maintenance taxes received in fiscal year 2015.

children.label: C
children.text: For fiscal year 2017, 71 percent of the total amount of harbor maintenance taxes received in fiscal year 2016.

children.label: D
children.text: For fiscal year 2018, 74 percent of the total amount of harbor maintenance taxes received in fiscal year 2017.

children.label: E
children.text: For fiscal year 2019, 77 percent of the total amount of harbor maintenance taxes received in fiscal year 2018.

children.label: F
children.text: For fiscal year 2020, 80 percent of the total amount of harbor maintenance taxes received in fiscal year 2019.

children.label: G
children.text: For fiscal year 2021, 83 percent of the total amount of harbor maintenance taxes received in fiscal year 2020.

children.label: H
children.text: For fiscal year 2022, 87 percent of the total amount of harbor maintenance taxes received in fiscal year 2021.

children.label: I
children.text: For fiscal year 2023, 91 percent of the total amount of harbor maintenance taxes received in fiscal year 2022.

children.label: J
children.text: For fiscal year 2024, 95 percent of the total amount of harbor maintenance taxes received in fiscal year 2023.

children.label: K
children.text: For fiscal year 2025, and each fiscal year thereafter, 100 percent of the total amount of harbor maintenance taxes received in the previous fiscal year.

children.heading: Use of amounts
children.label: 2 Use of amounts
children.text: The total budget resources described in paragraph (1) may be used only for making expenditures under .section 9505(c) of title 26
103 percent of the total budget resources appropriated for the previous fiscal year; or
100 percent of the total amount of harbor maintenance taxes received in the previous fiscal year.

children.heading: Sense of Congress
children.label: 1 Sense of Congress
children.text: It is the sense of Congress that any increase in funding for harbor maintenance programs under this section shall result from an overall increase in appropriations for the civil works program of the Corps of Engineers and not from reductions in the appropriations for other programs, projects, and activities carried out by the Corps of Engineers for other authorized purposes.

children.heading: Application
children.label: 2 Application
children.text: The target total budget resources for a fiscal year specified in subsection (b)(1) shall only apply in a fiscal year for which the level of appropriations provided for the civil works program of the Corps of Engineers in that fiscal year is increased, as compared to the previous fiscal year, by a dollar amount that is at least equivalent to the dollar amount necessary to address such target total budget resources in that fiscal year.
